---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis"
subtitle: ""
date: last-modified
sidebar: auto
number-sections: false
toc: true
author:
  - Jumbong Junior 

categories: []
tags: [""]
title-block-banner: false
format: 
  html: 
    mainfont: Times New Roman
    fontsize: 1.1em
execute: 
  cache: false

jupyter: python3

---

# Data Analysis


```{python}

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

import importlib

import importlib

import src.data_analysis as data_analysis
import src.schroders_chart4 as schroders_chart4
import src.ols_lm as ols_lm
import src.shiller_figure as shiller_figure
import src.cape_crisis_chart as cape_crisis_chart

importlib.reload(data_analysis)
importlib.reload(schroders_chart4)
importlib.reload(ols_lm)
importlib.reload(shiller_figure)
importlib.reload(cape_crisis_chart)

```


## Figure 1: CAPE Evolution since 1881.




```{python}
# Impoort data from datasets
data_shiller_with_date_in_string = pd.read_excel(f"{data_path}/shiller_with_date_in_string_format.xlsx")

# Convert date column to datetime format
data_shiller_with_date_in_string['date'] = pd.to_datetime(data_shiller_with_date_in_string['date'], format='%Y-%m-%d')

data_shiller_with_date_in_string.tail(1)
```

```{python}
# Select columns of interest : date, cape, real_10y_stock_return

data_shiller_cape = data_shiller_with_date_in_string[['date', 'cape', 'real_10y_stock_return']]
# Delete rows with missing values
data_shiller_cape = data_shiller_cape.dropna()
# log cape
data_shiller_cape['log_cape'] = np.log(data_shiller_cape['cape'])
print(data_shiller_cape.head() )
# last row of data_shiller_cape
print(data_shiller_cape.tail(1))

```


```{python}



# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Standalone demo
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if __name__ == "__main__":
    df = data_shiller_with_date_in_string.copy()

    fig = cape_crisis_chart.plot_cape_crises(df)
    # save in png
    
    fig.show()

    
   
```

## Figure 2: Lows in the 12-month trailling S&P P/E during recessions
```{python}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Standalone demo
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if __name__ == "__main__":
    df = data_shiller_with_date_in_string

    fig = schroders_chart4.plot_chart4(df)
    fig.show()

    # Print recession P/E lows table
    data = schroders_chart4.compute_recession_pe_lows(df)
    print(f"\nAverage P/E low : {data.attrs['pe_avg']:.2f}")
    print(f"Latest P/E      : {data.attrs['pe_latest']:.2f}")
    print(data[["label","pe_min"]].to_string(index=False))
```

## Stationnarity study of the CAPE ratio and real 10-year stock return
```{python}
# stationnarity test : Augmented Dickey-Fuller test
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller

def test_adfuller(series, series_name):
    result = adfuller(series)
    print(f"ADF Statistic for {series_name}: {result[0]:.4f}")
    print(f"p-value: {result[1]:.4f}")
    print("Critical Values:")
    for key, value in result[4].items():
        print(f"   {key}: {value:.4f}")

test_adfuller(data_shiller_cape['cape'], 'CAPE Ratio')
test_adfuller(data_shiller_cape['real_10y_stock_return'], 'Real 10-Year Stock Return')
# stationnarity test for log cape
test_adfuller(data_shiller_cape['log_cape'] , 'Log CAPE Ratio')
```


## Figure 3: Shiller CAPE Ratio and Actual and Forecast 10-Year Real Stock
Returns, 1881–2014
```{python}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Standalone demo
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
if __name__ == "__main__":

    df = data_shiller_cape.copy()
    d_train = df[
        (df["date"] >= "1881-01-01") & (df["date"] <= "2004-12-01")
    ].dropna(subset=["cape", "real_10y_stock_return"]).copy()

    d_test = df[df["date"] >= "2005-01-01"].dropna(subset=["cape"]).copy()

    result = ols_lm.lm(
        y          = d_train["real_10y_stock_return"],
        X          = np.log(d_train["cape"]),
        y_name     = "RET_t",
        x_names    = ["log(CAPE_t)"],
        X_forecast = np.log(d_test["cape"]),
    )

    print(result["equation"])

    fig = shiller_figure.plot_shiller_figure1(
        dates_train   = d_train["date"],
        actual_train  = d_train["real_10y_stock_return"] * 100,
        fitted_train  = result["fitted"],
        dates_test    = d_test["date"],
        actual_test   = d_test["real_10y_stock_return"] * 100,
        forecast_test = result["forecast"],
        cape          = pd.concat([d_train["cape"], d_test["cape"]]),
        dates_cape    = pd.concat([d_train["date"], d_test["date"]]),
    )

    fig.show()
    print("Saved → shiller_figure1.html")
```




## Figure 4 : Evolution of the CAPE ratio from 1881 to 2026.

```{python}

import importlib
import src.cape_pe10_chart as cape_pe10_chart
importlib.reload(cape_pe10_chart)


df   = data_shiller_with_date_in_string
fig  = cape_pe10_chart.plot_cape_pe10(df)
fig.show()



```



```{python}

import importlib
from src import cape_interest_chart
importlib.reload(cape_interest_chart)
from src.cape_interest_chart import *

df   = data_shiller_with_date_in_string

fig = plot_cape_interest(df)
fig.savefig("cape_interest.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.close()
print("Saved → cape_interest.png")
```

## Nvidia GRowth Stock Price and CAPE Ratio


```{python}
"""
nvidia_growth_chart.py
======================
NVIDIA annual revenue bar chart (FY2016-FY2026) in Schroders style.
Data sourced from official NVIDIA SEC filings & earnings releases.
"""

import plotly.graph_objects as go
import math

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data — NVIDIA fiscal year revenue ($B)
# Fiscal year ends late January (e.g. FY2026 ended Jan 25, 2026)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
fiscal_years = [
    "FY2016", "FY2017", "FY2018", "FY2019", "FY2020",
    "FY2021", "FY2022", "FY2023", "FY2024", "FY2025", "FY2026"
]
revenue_b = [5.01, 6.91, 9.71, 11.72, 10.92,
             16.68, 26.91, 26.97, 60.92, 130.50, 215.94]

def fmt(v):
    if v >= 100:
        return f"${v:.1f}B"
    elif v >= 10:
        return f"${v:.1f}B"
    else:
        return f"${v:.2f}B"

_NAVY  = "#1a2f5e"
_GREY  = "rgba(200,200,200,0.35)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Figure
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
fig = go.Figure()

fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x            = fiscal_years,
    y            = revenue_b,
    name         = "Revenue",
    marker_color = _NAVY,
    marker_line  = dict(width=0),
    text         = [fmt(v) for v in revenue_b],
    textposition = "outside",
    textfont     = dict(size=10, color=_NAVY, family="Arial Bold"),
    hovertemplate = "<b>%{x}</b><br>Revenue: %{text}<extra></extra>",
    width        = 0.6,
))

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Layout — log scale so all bars are visible
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
fig.update_layout(
    title = dict(
        text = (
            "<b>NVIDIA Revenue: The growth has been extraordinary</b><br>"
            "<sup style='color:grey'>Annual revenue FY2016–FY2026 · Source: NVIDIA SEC filings</sup>"
        ),
        font = dict(size=14, color=_NAVY, family="Arial"),
        x    = 0,
    ),
    plot_bgcolor  = "white",
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    yaxis = dict(
        type      = "log",
        range     = [0.6, 2.45],      # 10^0.6≈4B → 10^2.45≈280B
        tickvals  = [5, 10, 25, 50, 100, 200],
        ticktext  = ["$5B", "$10B", "$25B", "$50B", "$100B", "$200B"],
        showgrid  = True,
        gridcolor = _GREY,
        zeroline  = False,
        tickfont  = dict(size=11, color="#555"),
        title     = dict(
            text = "Annual Revenue — log scale (USD)",
            font = dict(size=11, color="#555"),
        ),
    ),
    xaxis = dict(
        showgrid = False,
        tickfont = dict(size=11, color="#222", family="Arial"),
        tickangle = -35,
    ),
    margin     = dict(l=80, r=50, t=90, b=80),
    width      = 750,
    height     = 500,
    bargap     = 0.3,
    showlegend = False,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Annotation — ×43 growth callout
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
fig.add_annotation(
    x         = "FY2026",
    y         = math.log10(215.94),
    text      = "<b>×43</b> vs FY2016",
    showarrow = True,
    arrowhead = 2,
    arrowcolor = _NAVY,
    ax = -90, ay = -36,
    font        = dict(size=11, color=_NAVY),
    bgcolor     = "rgba(255,255,255,0.88)",
    bordercolor = _NAVY,
    borderwidth = 1,
    borderpad   = 4,
)

# AI inflection point annotation
fig.add_annotation(
    x         = "FY2024",
    y         = math.log10(60.92),
    text      = "ChatGPT era →<br>AI demand surge",
    showarrow = True,
    arrowhead = 2,
    arrowcolor = "#c0392b",
    ax = -80, ay = -50,
    font        = dict(size=10, color="#c0392b"),
    bgcolor     = "rgba(255,255,255,0.85)",
    bordercolor = "#c0392b",
    borderwidth = 1,
    borderpad   = 3,
)

# Source note
fig.add_annotation(
    xref="paper", yref="paper",
    x=0, y=-0.18,
    text      = "Source: NVIDIA Corporation — Annual earnings releases & SEC 10-K filings (FY ends late January)",
    showarrow = False,
    font      = dict(size=9, color="grey"),
    align     = "left",
)

fig.show()
```
